channel.title: Python on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Python on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: Announcing: GitHub Copilot and Claude Code connectors in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes generally available connectors for GitHub Copilot and Claude Code. These connectors let .NET and Python agents delegate coding tasks to either system without custom adapter code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Multi-agent orchestration SDK with the Microsoft Foundry Agent Framework in C# and Python
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry has introduced Agent Framework, a unified multi-agent orchestration SDK for C# and Python. It consolidates orchestration patterns previously split across AutoGen and Semantic Kernel into a single set of abstractions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Tracing for Microsoft Agent Framework (Python and .NET)
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now provides public preview tracing support for Microsoft Agent Framework in Python and .NET. The feature lets developers observe agent reasoning, tool calls, and model interactions during execution.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Support for Python-2.7; 3.8 and PowerShell- 7.1; 7.2 will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure Automation will stop supporting Python 2.7, Python 3.8, and PowerShell 7.1/7.2 starting October 1, 2026. Runbooks may still run, but these runtimes will no longer receive security updates or bug fixes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Deprecation of Python 3.9 for Dependabot
item.description:
Dependabot no longer supports Python 3.9 because it has reached end of life. Continuing to use Python 3.9 may prevent Dependabot from creating pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Anyscale on Azure
item.description:
Anyscale on Azure is now in public preview. It provides a managed Ray platform for running Python workloads at scale on Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure AI Translator SDKs for the latest text translation API
item.description:
Azure AI Translator has released generally available SDKs for the latest text translation REST API. The GA SDKs are available for C#/.NET, Java, JavaScript, and Python.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure HorizonDB Agentic Advisor Solution Accelerator
item.description:
Azure HorizonDB Agentic Advisor Solution Accelerator is now generally available. It provides a financial services-focused reference solution for building multi-agent Python applications with LangGraph and Mem0 on Azure HorizonDB.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for June
item.description:
Azure SQL added support in the MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code to run SQL queries in SQL Notebooks, document notebooks with Markdown, and combine kernels such as Python with SQL. The update is generally available as of June 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Update: Microsoft Entra ID token refresh support for Python, .NET, and JavaScript in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Microsoft Entra ID token refresh is now available in Python, .NET, and JavaScript client libraries for Azure Database for PostgreSQL. This reduces the need to manually handle token renewal in applications.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: langchain-azure-cosmosdb python package for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description:
Microsoft announced general availability of the langchain-azure-cosmosdb Python package for Azure Cosmos DB. The package adds direct support for vector and hybrid search, semantic caching, and chat history for LangChain and LangGraph applications.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Python 3.9 for Dependabot
item.description:
Dependabot will stop supporting Python 3.9 on June 23, 2026 because Python 3.9 has reached end of life. Users still on 3.9 may see Dependabot issues after the cutoff.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework is now generally available at version 1.0 for .NET and Python. The release includes stable APIs, long-term support, multi-agent orchestration, multi-provider model support, and cross-runtime interoperability via A2A.

  • Source: Azure Updates
